seo.title: Camping Route - Prompt Generator & Pitch Search Europe
seo.description: Create strong prompts for camping routes in Europe with pitch search, campsite search, stage planning, and GPX export.
seo.keywords: AI camping route planner, campsite finder, motorhome pitch finder, camping finder, RV route planner, caravan route planner, tent road trip planner, motorcycle route planner, GPX route planner, campsite route planner, Camping Route, CampingRoute, camping routes

placeFinderDescriptions.camping: Dedicated search page for classic campsites.
placeFinderDescriptions.stopover: Dedicated search page for flexible motorhome stopovers.

hero.title: Camping Route
hero.description: Our AI prompt generator is your practical companion for finding the perfect prompts for your favorite AI. It helps you plan your tours with an RV, caravan, tent, or motorcycle more easily, including campsite and pitch search, stage planning, and GPX export.

promptGen.title: AI Prompt Generation
promptGen.description: Creates optimized prompts for your AI-supported route planning with motorhome-specific parameters.

smartFilters.title: Smart Filters
smartFilters.description: Filter by budget, vehicle size, interests, and pitch facilities for precise prompts.

readyInstantly.title: Ready instantly
readyInstantly.description: No registration required. Get your finished AI prompt in under 3 minutes.

privacyFirst.title: Privacy first
privacyFirst.description: No data storage. Everything remains locally in your browser.

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.description: Fully transparent. Code can be viewed and contributed to on GitHub.

freeAdFree.title: Free & Ad-free
freeAdFree.description: No hidden costs. No advertising. Completely free to use.

julia.name: Julia
julia.text: Perfect for spontaneous trips! Within minutes I had a detailed route with all the important info.

markus.name: Markus
markus.text: Finally a route planner that really caters to my needs! The AI suggested a perfect route for me.

sarah.name: Sarah
sarah.text: The filter options are brilliant! I can filter by budget and vehicle size – that saves so much time!

exampleRoute.title: Karlsruhe → Perleberg → Wismar → Karlsruhe
exampleRoute.subtitle: 12 days • approx. 1,350 km • Slow travel 3.5-7.5 t
exampleRoute.lead: Start on June 3, 2026, booked intermediate stops in Perleberg and Wismar, a daily limit of 250 km, and a return route that deliberately avoids mirroring the outbound trip.

ruleNotice.title: Travel note
items: June 4, 2026 is Corpus Christi in Baden-Wuerttemberg: expect heavier excursion traffic on the south-west axis toward Franconia and Hesse.

items.title: Stage 1: Karlsruhe → Obereisenheim / Wuerzburg
items.route: Easy start via A5 / A6 / A81 toward Wuerzburg. Kanu-Club Wuerzburg with OCM link https://opencampingmap.org/en/way/1190353250 and official site https://www.kc-wuerzburg.de/camping-gastst%C3%A4tte/
items.pause: Short lunch or coffee break near Heilbronn, then arrive early in the wine village.
items.note: Relaxed start before the holiday traffic.

items.title: Stage 2: Obereisenheim / Wuerzburg → Hann. Muenden / Knickhagen
items.route: Head north on the A7 toward the Fulda / Werra / Weser region. Campingplatz Knickhagen with site page https://www.campingplatz-knickhagen.de/Campingplatz/ and restaurant https://www.campingplatz-knickhagen.de/Die-Gaststaette/Speisekarte/
items.pause: Longer break around Fulda or Kassel to keep the holiday relaxed.
items.note: Very fitting evening stop with restaurant directly on site.

items.title: Stage 3: Knickhagen → Havelberg area
items.route: A7 / A38 / A14 / B189 with a calm eastern bypass. Campinginsel Havelberg with https://opencampingmap.org/de/way/211239954 and https://www.campinginsel-havelberg.de/
items.pause: Pause window south of Magdeburg or around Stendal.
items.note: Buffer day before the fixed date in Perleberg.

items.title: Stage 4: Havelberg / Perleberg area → Perleberg
items.route: Perleberg is fixed for June 6, 2026 with planned arrival around 13:00. This day therefore stays deliberately short and soft.
items.pause: Supplies, town time, or just a very short transfer instead of a full driving stage.

items.title: Stage 5: Perleberg → Wismar
items.route: Quiet northern route toward the Baltic Sea with arrival timed for the booked Wismar stay.
items.pause: Short stop near Schwerin or drive straight through to arrive in Wismar early.

stay.title: Stay on site
stay.note: Wismar is firmly booked. Strong base for the old town, old harbor, and World Heritage House: https://www.wismar-stralsund.de/en/experience_world_heritage/visitor_centres/world_heritage_house_wismar/ • more inspiration: https://one-million-places.com/en/germany/wismar-sights-of-the-unseco-hanseatic-city

items.title: Stage 6: Wismar → Lueneburg Heath
items.route: Do not return via the outbound track, but head southwest via country roads and smaller federal roads into the heath. Naturcamping Lueneburger Heide with OCM link https://opencampingmap.org/en/way/79293021 and campsite page https://naturcamping-lh.de/campingplatz/
items.pause: Break near Ludwigslust or Uelzen, then a relaxed rolling-in evening in the heath.

items.title: Stage 7: Lueneburg Heath → Guxhagen / Fuldaschleife
items.route: Via B4 / A7 back toward Hesse, but without copying the outbound route. For the last overnight stop: Camping Fuldaschleife with site page https://fuldaschleife.de/ and pitch selection https://fuldaschleife.de/stellplatz-auswahlen/
items.pause: Longer midday stop around Hildesheim or Goettingen to keep the north-south axis calm.

items.title: Stage 8: Guxhagen / Fuldaschleife → Karlsruhe
items.route: Final drive home via A7 / A6 / A5 back to Karlsruhe, without an overlong final stage.
items.pause: Short rest-area or truck-stop break near Heilbronn, then relaxed arrival home in the afternoon.

overnights.title: Overnights
overnights.note: This route uses only specifically named places and direct links.

items.title: June 3 • Mainfranken
items.name: Kanu-Club Wuerzburg / Obereisenheim
details: Calm first evening on the Main with wine villages and short reach to town or dinner options

items.title: June 4 • North Hesse
items.name: Campingplatz Knickhagen
details: Quiet stop near Hann. Muenden with a real dinner option directly on site

items.title: June 5 • Reserve before Perleberg
items.name: Campinginsel Havelberg
details: Clean reserve and buffer night before the fixed date in Perleberg

items.title: June 6 • Intermediate stop Perleberg
items.name: Booked stay
details: Deliberately planned as a short destination and buffer section instead of a full driving day
Havelberg remains the nearest fallback option

items.title: June 7-14 • Wismar
items.name: Booked stay
details: No campsite search needed, full focus on town, harbor, and day trips

outward.title: Outbound
items: Mainfranken around Obereisenheim: wine villages, Main riverbanks, and a gentle travel opening
Hann. Muenden / Weserstein as a strong stop on the way north
Havelberg Cathedral as a quality add-on or fallback before Perleberg: https://havelberg-dom.de/havelberg-dombesuch-info-oeffnungszeiten-lage/

onsite.title: On site
items: Wismar old town (UNESCO), old harbor, and Brick Gothic all within compact walking distance

return.title: Return
items: Lueneburg Heath as a quiet natural contrast to the Baltic coast and city days in Wismar
Fuldaschleife for one last relaxed river evening before driving home
The return route stays independent and deliberately avoids repeating the same north-south axis as the outbound trip

items.label: Navigation
items.value: Use a camper GPS with vehicle dimensions (L: 7.2 m • H: 3.2 m • 5.5 t), e.g. TomTom Go Camper or Garmin.

items.label: Waste
items.value: Clesana toilet = residual waste. Greywater disposal available at all listed sites.

items.label: Fresh water
items.value: Large tank; refilling possible at each station.

items.label: Emergency
items.value: Mark veterinary clinics along the route in advance (e.g. Magdeburg, Lüneburg).

items.label: Fuel
items.value: Truck stops are cheaper than motorway service areas. Use truck lanes if car pumps are too tight.

items.label: Groceries
items.value: Look for large parking lots in commercial areas (e.g. Kaufland, Globus).

items.label: Apps
items.value: Clever Tanken, Park4Night (filter >7 m, dog friendly), Promobil Stellplatz-Radar, NINA alerts.

items.label: Budget
items.value: Approx. €1,200–1,500 for fuel, pitches, food, and entry fees.

items.label: Sustainability
items.value: Solar & Clesana mean no chemicals. Dispose bags in residual waste and support local farm shops.

items.label: Health
items.value: Tick protection for the dog is essential in June; keep the vaccination record handy.

items.label: Best time
items.value: June is ideal: long days, Baltic Sea around 15–17 °C.

items.label: Checklist
items.value: Long CEE adapter cable, water hose + watering can, heavy-duty leveling ramps.

items.label: Tires/backup
items.value: Carry a tire repair kit for trucks/RVs.

items.label: Self-sufficient
items.value: 600W solar / 600Ah / Clesana allow flexible site choice.

items.label: Wild camping
items.value: Prohibited in Germany and strictly enforced in protected areas.

items.label: Rest break
items.value: One-off overnight on parking lots is tricky with 7.2 m length – use official pitches.

items.label: Options
items.value: Landvergnügen or farm stays are good spontaneous alternatives.

items.label: A7 outbound
items.value: Detour via B27 (Fulda → Göttingen) – scenic but slower.

items.label: Hamburg on return
items.value: Bypass Hamburg widely to the east (Uelzen/B4), avoid the Elbtunnel.

whatIs.title: The prompt generator for camping and road-trip routes.
whatIs.prec: What Camping Route does for you
whatIs.prec1: You enter your start, destination, travel dates, vehicle, and preferences once in a clean structure.
whatIs.prec2: Camping Route turns that into a well-structured prompt for ChatGPT, Claude, or other AI tools.
whatIs.prec3: With the place finder, you can pick matching campsites or stopovers first and transfer them directly.
whatIs.ai1: Less back-and-forth inside the AI chat.
whatIs.ai2: Better instructions for stages, overnight stops, and vehicle logic.
whatIs.ai3: A faster path from travel idea to a useful route prompt.

diff.q: Prompt vs. Direct AI – what's the difference?
diff.title: Either copy for free or generate directly in the app.
diff.gen2: Paste it into ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini.
diff.gen3: Free, using your existing AI account.
diff.gen4: You keep full control of the follow-up chat.
diff.ai: Mode: Use AI Directly (API Key needed)
diff.ai1: The route is generated and shown directly in the app.
diff.ai4: You need your own API key from your AI provider.

howItWorks.q: How exactly does the planning work?
howItWorks.title: From destination to finished route prompt in three steps.
howItWorks.step1a: Set your start, destination, travel window, daily limit, and any stopovers.
howItWorks.step2a: Add vehicle details and optionally transfer matching campsites or stopovers.
howItWorks.step3a: Copy the finished prompt and continue in your preferred AI tool.

exampleRoute.q: What does an example route look like?
exampleRoute.title: A concrete example with stages, stops, and overnight suggestions.

aiModel.q: How do the place finder and prompt generator work together?
aiModel.title: Places you find can go straight into your planning flow.
aiModel.gpt1: In the embedded place finder, you can set results directly as destination or stopover.
aiModel.gpt2: Existing stops are recognized so you can replace or extend them without guesswork.
aiModel.prompt1: The campsite finder and stopover finder can also transfer results straight into your current generator draft.
aiModel.recDesc: Your current draft stays in the same browser tab when you switch between finder and generator.

cost.q: What does Camping Route actually cost me?
cost.title: Camping Route itself is free.
cost.free1: The form and prompt generation are 100% free.
cost.free2: No sign-up required. You can use the prompt generator directly in your browser.
cost.transDesc: Costs only arise if you use the generated prompt in a paid AI service. Those costs are charged solely by your chosen provider, not by Camping Route.

privacy.q: Where is my entered data stored?
privacy.title: On your device by default.
privacy.device1: There is no central user account for your plans and no server-side plan storage. Drafts and saved inputs stay locally in your browser or in your exported file.
privacy.sec1: Your data only leaves your device when you paste the generated prompt into an external AI service yourself.

helpImprove.q: Can I help make Camping Route better?
helpImprove.title: Yes. Feedback, ideas, and contributions help directly.
helpImprove.content1: Send feedback directly on the site or share ideas for improvements.
helpImprove.content2: Report bugs or unclear parts so we can fix them faster.
helpImprove.content3: If you want to contribute technically, you can find the source code on GitHub.

planner.title: Everything for your prompt in one place

launcher.title: The prompt generator for camping and road trips
launcher.description: Open the generator, search suitable places if needed and then complete your route details and AI prompt in one strong flow.
launcher.hint: Place search is available directly inside the generator.

loading.googleUnavailable: Gemini is currently under high demand. Please try again shortly or temporarily switch to another provider.

save.title: Save inputs locally
save.desc: Stores your form inputs on this device for 30 days. Use “Clear data” to remove the saved data from this device.
save.note: Plans can also be exported locally as a file and imported again later.
save.coffeeHint: CampingRoute.app is open-source and ad-free — your support helps with server and development costs. Thank you!

savedPlans.title: 5 recent plans
savedPlans.desc: Stores up to 5 plans locally in the browser. You can load, overwrite or delete them later.
savedPlans.duplicated: Plan saved and loaded as a variant.
savedPlans.exported: Plan exported locally as a file.
savedPlans.apiKeyMissing: A valid API key is required to generate a route.

steps.hint: Adjust each section directly in the form.
steps.routeIncomplete: Please fill in start, destination, and every added stop.

ai.title: AI Settings
ai.description: Generate a finished prompt for your preferred AI.

direct.title: Use AI Directly
direct.desc: Direct AI output with your API key.

mode.label: How would you like to plan?
mode.trigger: Choose how you want to interact with AI.

apiKey.hint: Your key is not stored and is used only for this session.
apiKey.security: Camping Route does not store your API key and does not process it server-side. It stays only in this browser session and is sent encrypted directly to the selected AI provider.

route.title: Itinerary
route.description: Set the details of your trip.

gpx.label: GPX Output
gpx.description: Choose which GPX file(s) you want at the end.

destinationBooked.label: Place at destination already booked
destinationBooked.description: If enabled, AI should not search for an overnight place at the destination.

stage.label: Stop {{num}}
stage.bookedDescription: If enabled, AI should not search for an overnight place at this stop.
stage.detailsDescription: Optionally define arrival and departure for this stop.

targetRegions.label: Regions & target areas
targetRegions.hint: For round trips from home, you can set start and destination to the same place and define only regions or landscapes here.
targetRegions.placeholder: e.g. Moselle, South Tyrol, Brittany, North Sea coast or Black Forest

quickStart.label: Quick start
quickStart.description: Apply common route preferences with one click and see which choices are already active.

additional.label: Additional Info
additional.placeholder: e.g. Plan the return trip on a different route than the outbound trip

preferScenicLongerStops.label: Longer stops at especially beautiful places
preferScenicLongerStops.description: The AI should extend stays where 2-3 nights are especially worthwhile.

flexibleDuration.label: Flexible trip duration
flexibleDuration.description: AI may adjust duration/stages if helpful.

route.stageHint: Stage destinations only for staged journeys

limitPriority.label: What matters more?
limitPriority.description: If both daily limits are set, the AI can prioritize the more important one.

travelPace.label: Travel pace (quick pick)
travelPace.note: Max km/day and drive time/day take priority; stage style is a soft preference.

destinationStay.label: Stay at a holiday destination
destinationStay.description: Plan outbound travel, the stay, and the return trip around a fixed holiday destination.

route.destinationArrivalTime: Desired arrival time at the vacation destination

stages.title: Stops

stages.required: A place is required as soon as a stop is added.

destinationDetails.label: Plan times in more detail

roundTripHint.text: For round trips: set start and destination to the same place, then add regions below.

placeFinder.title: Place Finder

feedback.title: How does Camping Route feel?

description.prompt: You just generated a prompt. Two clicks help me improve the product in the right places.
description.route: You just generated a route. Two clicks help me judge quality and usefulness much better.

options.helpfulHint: The result was solid and usable.
options.notHelpfulHint: Something was unclear, off, or missing.

feedback.messagePlaceholder: For example: clearer prompts, better stage logic, more helpful guidance, or easier handling ...
feedback.privacy: Please do not enter personal data. Feedback is stored only for product improvement.

disclaimer.title: Liability for Content and Links
disclaimer.description1: As a service provider, we are responsible for our own content on these pages according to § 7 Abs.1 TMG. The generated motorhome routes are created by external Artificial Intelligence (such as OpenAI or Gemini). We assume no liability or guarantee for the accuracy, feasibility, or safety of the suggested routes and overnight stops. Using these routes is strictly at the driver's own risk and responsibility.
disclaimer.description2: Our service contains links to external third-party websites. We have no influence on their content and therefore cannot assume any liability for these external contents.

openSource.title: Copyright & Open Source
openSource.description1: The content and works created by the site operators on these pages are subject to the MIT License. The project is open-source. You can view, modify, and use the source code on GitHub.

general.title: General Data Processing
general.description: Protecting your data is very important to us. CampingRoute.app works primarily client-side and does not use user accounts or central route storage. For selected features, server-side proxy requests to public data services may be used. Your route planning inputs remain primarily in your browser.

local.title: Local Storage
local.description: If you enable local saving, your form inputs and settings (e.g., chosen language) are stored only in your browser and are automatically deleted after 30 days. API keys are never stored. This data is never transmitted to our servers.

ai.title: AI Integration (OpenAI, Gemini, Mistral)
ai.description: If you use direct AI generation, your form data (start, destination, vehicle, preferences) is converted into text (prompt) and sent directly to your chosen AI provider (OpenAI, Google Gemini, or Mistral AI) using your own API key. We do not store this traffic or log API keys. The privacy policies of the AI provider you use apply.

places.title: Place search & OSM data
places.description: If you use the place finder on the home page or inside the route planner, your search term and technically necessary connection data are forwarded server-side to Geoapify or, as a fallback, to public Nominatim and Overpass services based on OpenStreetMap in order to find places as well as matching campsites and stopovers. We use these search requests only to answer your search directly and do not keep a permanent search history on our side. If you open the map view, your browser also loads map tiles from the configured map provider, which receives technically necessary connection data such as your IP address.

feedback.title: Feedback after generation
feedback.description: After a route or prompt has been generated successfully, we may optionally ask you for short product feedback. We only store your rating (for example helpful / not helpful), an optional message, technical metadata such as language, selected mode, AI provider or model, and a timestamp. Please do not enter personal or sensitive data in free-text fields. Processing is carried out solely to improve CampingRoute.app; no disclosure to third parties takes place.

sidebar.description: CampingRoute.app does not use tracking cookies that require consent (like Google Analytics, Meta Pixel, etc.). We do not use any mechanisms to analyze your user behavior.

noTracking.description: Only essential, technically necessary information for the React app to function (e.g. for multi-language support via i18next) is kept locally in the browser.

rights.title: Your Rights
rights.description: Since we do not store personal data on our servers, the traditional right to deletion or information requests from us does not apply. You have full control over your data at all times: to remove all settings saved by CampingRoute.app, simply clear your browser data (Local Storage) for this website.

hosting.title: Hosting
hosting.description: This website is hosted on a cloud server provided by Hetzner Online GmbH. When accessing this site, the hosting provider automatically collects access data (server log files) such as IP address, browser used, and time of access. This data is only used for the secure operation of the servers and is not merged with other data sources.

prompt.systemRole: You are a professional route planner for camping and road-trip travel with specialized knowledge of Europe and international destinations. Create a practical, well-structured route based on my specifications that fits the selected vehicle type. Consider vehicle specifications, travel destinations, budget requirements, and personal preferences. If real-time data (e.g., traffic, availability, exact prices) is not available, work with plausible estimates, mark them as such, and indicate what I should check myself in the navigation system or a camping app.
prompt.dataSourcePolicy: Overnight place lookup must be primarily sourced from OpenCampingMap. OpenStreetMap is used only as an enrichment source for category hints (for example `caravan_site:type=farm|winery`) and is not preferred over OpenCampingMap.
prompt.accommodationTypeTagPolicy: Use OSM tags as a soft filter within OpenCampingMap hits for accommodation types: `camping` -> `tourism=camp_site`, `pitch` -> `tourism=caravan_site`, `farm` -> prefer `caravan_site:type=farm|winery`; for `small`, `wild`, and `premium`, use available tags plus name/description/website heuristics. If tags are missing or inconsistent, do not automatically exclude the place; mark the classification as an estimate.

labels.accommodationTypePriorityNote: No prioritization between selected accommodation types; treat them equally.
labels.budgetNote: Max budget per night is the hard ceiling. The place comfort level helps the AI choose more appropriately within that limit.
labels.travelPaceNote: soft preference, km/day and drive time/day take priority
labels.destinationArrivalTime: Arrival time at the vacation destination
labels.destinationBookedNoSearch: Destination place already booked (no search for overnight place at destination)
labels.preferScenicLongerStops: Plan longer stops at especially beautiful places
labels.returnScheduleHint: Use this to calculate when departure from the starting point is needed and when arrival at the return destination is realistic
labels.stageBookedNoSearch: Stop {{num}}: place already booked (no search for overnight place at this stop)

prompt.instructions:
Plan the route with the parameters mentioned above and structure your response into modules with clear headings. Do not use asterisks (*) or double asterisks (**) in the response.

1) Stage Planning (Daily kilometers, driving time, planned departure and arrival times for each leg, breaks every 2-3 hours, alternative routes if applicable, and brief justification).

2) Overnight Stays (use data exclusively from https://opencampingmap.org. Only list places that can be found there. For each suggested main place and each alternative, provide two links: 1) the OpenCampingMap page link and 2) the official campsite link stored there (if present). Provide one working OpenCampingMap link per place (direct place link or map link). If either link is missing, state that clearly. Do not invent places, links, addresses, or phone numbers. Mention price ranges only as estimates and still provide at least one alternative per overnight stay.)

3) Highlights & Activities (Top 3 per stage, briefly with costs/opening hours if known).

4) Practical Tips (Navigation, waste disposal, emergency information).

5) Best Travel Time & Duration (Brief section).

6) Services on the Road (Fuel, supermarkets, workshops).

7) Additional Info (Budget guidance, sustainability, health, connectivity).

8) Technology & Equipment (Short checklist, app recommendations).

9) Flexibility (Alternative routes, legal notes on wild camping, tools for further optimization).

Important: For each leg, clearly state from which place, on which date, and at what time departure is planned, and at which place, on which date, and at what time arrival is planned. If I provide only arrival dates/times or only departure dates/times, calculate the missing times logically from the remaining inputs, daily limits, and stages. Present these times as a realistic plan or estimate.

Important: Use your internal map knowledge, if available, to roughly calculate distances and typical driving times between stages. Provide distances in km and driving times in hours/minutes, and mark them as estimates (e.g., "approx. 230 km / 3:00-3:30 h"). Avoid exact precision if you do not have access to current routing data, and explicitly ask me to verify the route in the navigation system (e.g., Google Maps, Here, Garmin, OpenStreetMap app).

If your platform offers a file download, always save the GPX file with the .gpx extension (not .txt). If no download is possible, output only the GPX file, without any extra text or markers. Also provide a short download guide for macOS, iOS, Windows, and Android.
prompt.instructionsCamperPlanning:
Important: Rate each leg briefly from the perspective of the selected vehicle type, for example as "uncritical", "use caution" or "rather unsuitable for this vehicle", and explain that briefly.

Important: Include vehicle-relevant supply and service logic. Point out when fresh water, waste disposal, fuel, groceries, power, sanitary stops, or other suitable services should sensibly be planned.

Important: Classify suggested overnight places briefly, for example as a transit stop, a good 2-3 night stay, or a proper holiday base. If I specify regions/target areas or ask for longer stops at especially beautiful places, deliberately spend more time there instead of creating as many location changes as possible. Use these three terms verbatim whenever possible.

Important: For each leg, if possible, include a separate line such as "Rating: uncritical" or "Rating: use caution" or "Rating: rather unsuitable" plus a short reason, so the assessment stays easy to detect.

gpx.garmin:
GPX file: Append exactly one GPX file for Garmin waypoints at the end of the response.

Important: Output the GPX as a Markdown code block with xml (```xml ... ```), without additional markers like BEGIN/END.

```xml
<gpx version="1.1" creator="CampingRoute.app" xmlns="http://www.topografix.com/GPX/1/1">
  ...
</gpx>
```

Requirements:
Create <wpt> waypoints for start, all stages, overnight stays, and destination. For overnight stays, include address, website, and phone (if available) in <desc>. Alternatives (mandatory) must be listed only in <desc> with name, address, website, and phone (if available).
Do not output a route (<rte>) or a track (<trk>).

First return the complete normal answer with all required sections. Only after that append the GPX block at the very end. No text may follow after the GPX block.
gpx.routeTrack:
GPX file: Append exactly one GPX file (route+track) at the end of the response.

Important: Output the GPX as a Markdown code block with xml (```xml ... ```), without additional markers like BEGIN/END.

```xml
<gpx version="1.1" creator="CampingRoute.app" xmlns="http://www.topografix.com/GPX/1/1">
  ...
</gpx>
```

Requirements:
Create <wpt> waypoints for start, all stages, overnight stays, and destination. For overnight stays, include address, website, and phone (if available) in <desc>. Alternatives (mandatory) must be listed only in <desc> with name, address, website, and phone (if available).
Create a route (<rte>) with all points in order (<rtept>) so Garmin recognizes it as a route.
Also create the full route as a track (<trk> with <trkseg> and <trkpt>).

First return the complete normal answer with all required sections. Only after that append the GPX block at the very end. No text may follow after the GPX block.
gpx.both:
GPX file: Append exactly two GPX files at the end of the response.

Important: Return only two XML Markdown code blocks with xml (```xml ... ```), with no intro text, no labels, and no additional markers like BEGIN/END.

Block 1 (Garmin waypoints):
```xml
<gpx version="1.1" creator="CampingRoute.app" xmlns="http://www.topografix.com/GPX/1/1">
  ...
</gpx>
```

Separate block 1 and block 2 with at least one blank line.

Block 2 (GPX route+track):
```xml
<gpx version="1.1" creator="CampingRoute.app" xmlns="http://www.topografix.com/GPX/1/1">
  ...
</gpx>
```

Requirements for block 1 (Garmin waypoints):
Create <wpt> waypoints for start, all stages, overnight stays, and destination. For overnight stays, include address, website, and phone (if available) in <desc>. Alternatives (mandatory) must be listed only in <desc> with name, address, website, and phone (if available).
Block 1 may contain only <wpt> and must not output a route (<rte>) or a track (<trk>).

Requirements for block 2 (GPX route+track):
Create <wpt> waypoints for start, all stages, overnight stays, and destination. For overnight stays, include address, website, and phone (if available) in <desc>. Alternatives (mandatory) must be listed only in <desc> with name, address, website, and phone (if available).
Block 2 must contain <wpt>, <rte>, and <trk>. Generate a route (<rte>) with all points in order (<rtept>) so Garmin recognizes it as a route. Also create the full route as a track (<trk> with <trkseg> and <trkpt>).

First return the complete normal answer with all required sections. Only after that append these two GPX blocks at the very end. No text may follow after the GPX blocks.

gpxPlain.garmin:
GPX file: Append exactly one GPX file for Garmin waypoints at the end of the response.

Important: Output the GPX as a plain XML block, without Markdown, without backticks, and without additional markers like BEGIN/END.

<gpx version="1.1" creator="CampingRoute.app" xmlns="http://www.topografix.com/GPX/1/1">
  ...
</gpx>

Requirements:
Create <wpt> waypoints for start, all stages, overnight stays, and destination. For overnight stays, include address, website, and phone (if available) in <desc>. Alternatives (mandatory) must be listed only in <desc> with name, address, website, and phone (if available).
Do not output a route (<rte>) or a track (<trk>).

First return the complete normal answer with all required sections. Only after that append the GPX block at the very end. No text may follow after the GPX block.

<gpx version="1.1" creator="CampingRoute.app" xmlns="http://www.topografix.com/GPX/1/1">
  ...

Requirements:

Create a route (<rte>) with all points in order (<rtept>) so Garmin recognizes it as a route.
Also create the full route as a track (<trk> with <trkseg> and <trkpt>).

First return the complete normal answer with all required sections. Only after that append the GPX block at the very end. No text may follow after the GPX block.
gpxPlain.both:
GPX file: Append exactly two GPX files at the end of the response.

Important: Return only two plain XML blocks, with no intro text, no labels, no Markdown, no backticks, and no additional markers like BEGIN/END.

Block 1 (Garmin waypoints):
<gpx version="1.1" creator="CampingRoute.app" xmlns="http://www.topografix.com/GPX/1/1">
  ...
</gpx>

Separate block 1 and block 2 with at least one blank line.

Block 2 (GPX route+track):
<gpx version="1.1" creator="CampingRoute.app" xmlns="http://www.topografix.com/GPX/1/1">
  ...
</gpx>

Requirements for block 1 (Garmin waypoints):
Create <wpt> waypoints for start, all stages, overnight stays, and destination. For overnight stays, include address, website, and phone (if available) in <desc>. Alternatives (mandatory) must be listed only in <desc> with name, address, website, and phone (if available).
Block 1 may contain only <wpt> and must not output a route (<rte>) or a track (<trk>).

Requirements for block 2 (GPX route+track):
Create <wpt> waypoints for start, all stages, overnight stays, and destination. For overnight stays, include address, website, and phone (if available) in <desc>. Alternatives (mandatory) must be listed only in <desc> with name, address, website, and phone (if available).
Block 2 must contain <wpt>, <rte>, and <trk>. Generate a route (<rte>) with all points in order (<rtept>) so Garmin recognizes it as a route. Also create the full route as a track (<trk> with <trkseg> and <trkpt>).

First return the complete normal answer with all required sections. Only after that append these two GPX blocks at the very end. No text may follow after the GPX blocks.

app.reloadNotice: The page was automatically reloaded after an update.
app.updateAvailable: An update is available. Please reload the page.

whatsNew.title: What's new in {{version}}?
whatsNew.description: The main changes in the current release appear only once per version.

items.plannerPanel: Campsite and stopover finders now show results directly on a map with linked list and place details.
items.placeFinder: Place autocomplete now runs through Geoapify and gives much steadier suggestions than the old Nominatim path.
items.savedPlans: A local place index can prefill frequently used regions and noticeably speed up campsite and stopover search.
items.exampleRoute: Prepared Europe region files and a tougher refresh workflow now help build the local fast-search index.
